title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Traditional variable selection methods (e.g., backward elimination, LASSO) optimise statistical performance metrics AUC deviance. However, best-performing model statistical terms necessarily yield best clinical utility predictor costs (test harms) accounted . NBvarsel package implements variable selection framework based cross-validated Net Benefit, standard metric clinical utility decision curve analysis. core function nb_varsel() evaluates predictor subsets contribution clinical decision-making, optionally adjusted test harms. supports: Exhaustive groupwise (backward elimination) search strategies Predictor costs (per-variable grouped) Restricted cubic splines continuous predictors Interaction terms Permutation importance scores Parallel computation

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Reproducible section section fully reproducible data generation included code. Let us consider hypothetical scenario four predictors, two continuous (X1X_1, X3X_3) two binary (X2X_2, X4X_4), used predict binary outcome YY. true data-generating model : logit(Y)=−3+3X1+2X2+0.1X3−0.05X4(1)\text{logit}(Y) = -3 + 3X_1 + 2X_2 + 0.1X_3 - 0.05X_4 \qquad(1) model, X1X_1 X2X_2 strongly predictive, X3X_3 X4X_4 weakly predictive, outcome prevalence approximately 64%. predictor associated test harm: X1X_1 X3X_3 (harm = 0.05), X2X_2 (harm = 0.025), X4X_4 (harm = 0.00005). Using backward elimination based Wald’s statistic, implemented fastbw() rms package: Table 1: Results simple illustration. Models ranked cost-adjusted Net Benefit. variable importance feature shown Figure 1, illustrating main contributors utility. Figure 2, -subset plot shows evolution average adjusted Net Benefit across possible combinations. immediately apparent including X1X_1 improves performance. simple illustration demonstrates best subset predictors statistical terms necessarily translate better cost-utility. Figure 1: Net benefit based variable importance simple illustration. Figure 2: subset plot simple illustration.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 1000  X1 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1) X2 <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.7) X3 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1) X4 <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)  log_odds <- -3 + 3 * X1 + 2 * X2 + 0.1 * X3 - 0.05 * X4 prob <- plogis(log_odds) Y <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = prob)  df <- data.frame(X1 = X1, X2 = X2, X3 = X3, X4 = X4, Y = Y)  harms <- c(X1 = 0.05, X2 = 0.025, X3 = 0.05, X4 = 0.00005) model_full <- lrm(Y ~ X1 + X2 + X3 + X4, data = df) bw_result <- fastbw(model_full) sprintf("Backward elimination retains: %s", paste(bw_result$names.kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Backward elimination retains: X1, X2" exhaustive <- nb_varsel(   data = df,   outcome_var = "Y",   include_interactions = FALSE,   costs = harms,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "exhaustive",   thresholds = 0.5,   allow_parallel = FALSE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   n_knots = 3,   verbose = FALSE ) tbl_train <- exhaustive$all_models  tbl_train |>   select(Model, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit) |>   arrange(-Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit) |>   gt(id = "tb-train") |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Net Benefit",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. Net Benefit"   ) VIF_plot(tbl_train) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1))  all_subset_plot(   tbl_train,   filter = 100,   metric = "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit",   y_axis = "Adjusted Net Benefit" )

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
code: set.seed(42) n <- 1000  X1 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1) X2 <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.7) X3 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1) X4 <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = 0.5)  log_odds <- -3 + 3 * X1 + 2 * X2 + 0.1 * X3 - 0.05 * X4 prob <- plogis(log_odds) Y <- rbinom(n, size = 1, prob = prob)  df <- data.frame(X1 = X1, X2 = X2, X3 = X3, X4 = X4, Y = Y)  harms <- c(X1 = 0.05, X2 = 0.025, X3 = 0.05, X4 = 0.00005)

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Using backward elimination based Wald’s statistic, implemented fastbw() rms package:
code: model_full <- lrm(Y ~ X1 + X2 + X3 + X4, data = df) bw_result <- fastbw(model_full) sprintf("Backward elimination retains: %s", paste(bw_result$names.kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Backward elimination retains: X1, X2"

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
code: exhaustive <- nb_varsel(   data = df,   outcome_var = "Y",   include_interactions = FALSE,   costs = harms,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "exhaustive",   thresholds = 0.5,   allow_parallel = FALSE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   n_knots = 3,   verbose = FALSE )

Net Benefit",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. Net Benefit"   ) VIF_plot(tbl_train) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1))  all_subset_plot(   tbl_train,   filter = 100,   metric = "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit",   y_axis = "Adjusted Net Benefit" )

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Reproducible section section uses fabricated data designed resemble clinical prediction setting. data generation code included. now demonstrate methodology realistic scenario. simulate dataset mimicking heart failure readmission prediction setting 10 predictors binary outcome (readmitted within 30 days vs. ). Predictors grouped three cost categories: Clinical history (cost): patient age, prior admission, specialist centre, symptom severity Cardiac imaging (moderate cost): ejection fraction, wall thickness ratio, valve abnormalities, pericardial effusion, chamber dilation Blood biomarker (higher cost): NT-proBNP Costs defined grouped costs. group cost added predictor group included model. Table 2: Best model exhaustive search Table 3: models exhaustive search Figure 3: Variable importance plot (exhaustive search) Figure 3 shows variable importance based average Net Benefit contribution. Predictors ranked least important. Figure 4: subset plot (Net Benefit) Figure 5: subset plot (Adjusted Net Benefit) Figure 4 Figure 5 show -subset plots. point represents model. heatmap indicates predictors included. Note difference ranking costs accounted . Table 4: Groupwise selection: best model Figure 6: Variable importance (groupwise selection) compare NB-based variable selection backward elimination (Wald statistic) LASSO (L1-penalised logistic regression). Table 5: AUC test data variable selection method Figure 7: Decision curve analysis comparing net benefit across methods. Figure 8: Decision curve analysis comparing harm-adjusted net benefit. Figure 7 shows standard decision curve analysis. Figure 8 shows curves subtracting method’s test costs, highlighting advantage cost-aware variable selection.
code: # Clean up simple illustration objects to avoid namespace conflicts rm(list = setdiff(ls(), lsf.str()))  set.seed(8156) n_train <- 3000 n_test <- 1500 n_total <- n_train + n_test  # Clinical history (free) patient_age <- round(rnorm(n_total, mean = 68, sd = 12)) prior_admission <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.30) specialist_centre <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.45) symptom_severity <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.40)  # Cardiac imaging (moderate cost) ejection_fraction <- pmin(75, pmax(10, rnorm(n_total, 42, 14))) wall_thickness <- pmin(1, pmax(0.05, rbeta(n_total, 2, 4))) valve_abnormalities <- rpois(n_total, lambda = 0.8) pericardial_effusion <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.12) chamber_dilation <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.22)  # Blood biomarker (higher cost) nt_probnp <- round(pmax(20, rlnorm(n_total, meanlog = 6.5, sdlog = 1.4)))  # True model — strong signal for key predictors lp <- -16.0 +   0.07 * patient_age +   3.0 * prior_admission +   -0.12 * ejection_fraction +   10.0 * wall_thickness +   1.5 * valve_abnormalities +   2.5 * pericardial_effusion +   1.8 * chamber_dilation +   0.9 * log2(nt_probnp) +   # Weak / null predictors:   0.03 * specialist_centre +   0.02 * symptom_severity  readmitted <- rbinom(n_total, 1, plogis(lp))  clinical_df <- data.frame(   readmitted, patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission,   specialist_centre, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities,   pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, symptom_severity,   wall_thickness )  training_data <- clinical_df[1:n_train, ] test_data <- clinical_df[(n_train + 1):n_total, ]  sprintf("Training prevalence: %.1f%%", 100 * mean(training_data$readmitted)) [1] "Training prevalence: 34.5%" sprintf("Test prevalence: %.1f%%", 100 * mean(test_data$readmitted)) [1] "Test prevalence: 35.8%" prevalence <- mean(test_data$readmitted)  grouped_costs <- list(   history = list(     cost = 0,     vars = c("patient_age", "prior_admission", "specialist_centre",              "symptom_severity")   ),   imaging = list(     cost = prevalence * 0.03,     vars = c(       "ejection_fraction", "wall_thickness",       "valve_abnormalities", "pericardial_effusion", "chamber_dilation"     )   ),   biomarker = list(     cost = prevalence * 0.08,     vars = c("nt_probnp")   ) ) vars <- names(training_data)  start_time <- Sys.time() exhaustive_results <- nb_varsel(   data = training_data,   outcome_var = "readmitted",   costs = grouped_costs,   thresholds = seq(0.01, 0.2, by = 0.01),   include_interactions = FALSE,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "exhaustive",   allow_parallel = TRUE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE ) end_time <- Sys.time()  sprintf("Exhaustive search took: %s", format(end_time - start_time)) [1] "Exhaustive search took: 4.199386 mins" exhaustive_results$best_model_stats |>   select(Model, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit) |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Net Benefit",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. Net Benefit"   ) all_models <- exhaustive_results$all_models all_models$Rank_NB <- rank(-all_models$Avg_Net_Benefit, ties.method = "min") all_models$Rank_adj_NB <- rank(-all_models$Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, ties.method = "min")  tbl_data <- all_models |>   select(     Rank_NB, Rank_adj_NB, Model, AUC, Brier,     Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit   )  tbl <- tbl_data |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Rank_NB = "Rank (NB)",     Rank_adj_NB = "Rank (Adj. NB)",     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. NB",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. NB"   )  if (knitr::is_html_output()) {   tbl <- tbl |>     opt_interactive(       use_pagination = TRUE,       page_size_default = 10,       use_filters = TRUE,       use_compact_mode = TRUE     ) } tbl VIF_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = round(nrow(exhaustive_results$all_models) * 0.1) ) all_subset_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = 7,   size_dot = 1 )  all_subset_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = 7,   size_dot = 1,   metric = "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit",   y_axis = "Adjusted Net Benefit" ) start_time <- Sys.time() groupwise <- nb_varsel(   data = training_data,   outcome_var = "readmitted",   costs = grouped_costs,   thresholds = seq(0.01, 0.2, by = 0.01),   include_interactions = FALSE,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "groupwise",   allow_parallel = TRUE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   group_size = 1,   verbose = TRUE ) end_time <- Sys.time() sprintf("Groupwise search took: %s", format(end_time - start_time)) [1] "Groupwise search took: 17.77182 secs" groupwise$best_model_stats |>   select(Model, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit) |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. NB",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. NB"   ) VIF_plot(groupwise$all_models) preds <- setdiff(vars, "readmitted")  # --- Backward elimination --- fmla_full <- reformulate(termlabels = preds, response = "readmitted") model_full <- lrm(fmla_full, data = training_data) bw <- fastbw(model_full) model_bw <- lrm(   reformulate(termlabels = bw$names.kept, response = "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) test_data$bw_pred <- plogis(predict(model_bw, newdata = test_data))  # --- LASSO --- x_train <- as.matrix(training_data[, preds]) y_train <- training_data$readmitted x_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, preds])  set.seed(4738) cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, family = "binomial") lasso_coefs <- coef(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.1se") lasso_kept <- rownames(lasso_coefs)[as.vector(lasso_coefs != 0)] lasso_kept <- setdiff(lasso_kept, "(Intercept)") test_data$lasso_pred <- as.vector(   plogis(predict(cv_lasso, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.1se")) )  # --- NB models --- preds_best_nb <- str_trim(   str_split(     all_models |> arrange(-Avg_Net_Benefit) |> slice(1) |> pull(Model),     ","   )[[1]] ) preds_best_adj <- str_trim(   str_split(exhaustive_results$best_model_stats$Model, ",")[[1]] )  model_nb <- lrm(   reformulate(preds_best_nb, "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) model_adj_nb <- lrm(   reformulate(preds_best_adj, "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) test_data$nb_pred <- plogis(predict(model_nb, newdata = test_data)) test_data$adj_nb_pred <- plogis(predict(model_adj_nb, newdata = test_data)) sprintf("NB model retains: %s", paste(preds_best_nb, collapse = ", ")) [1] "NB model retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, specialist_centre, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("Adjusted NB model retains: %s", paste(preds_best_adj, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Adjusted NB model retains: patient_age, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("Backward elimination retains: %s", paste(bw$names.kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Backward elimination retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("LASSO retains: %s", paste(lasso_kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "LASSO retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, symptom_severity, wall_thickness" auc_results <- data.frame(   Method = c("NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination", "LASSO"),   AUC = c(     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$adj_nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$bw_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$lasso_pred))   ),   n_predictors = c(     length(preds_best_nb),     length(preds_best_adj),     length(bw$names.kept),     length(lasso_kept)   ) )  auc_results |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(columns = "AUC", decimals = 3) |>   cols_label(n_predictors = "N predictors") thresholds <- seq(0.01, 0.30, by = 0.005) n_test_obs <- nrow(test_data) y_test <- test_data$readmitted prev <- mean(y_test)  calc_nb <- function(probs, y, thresholds) {   n <- length(y)   vapply(thresholds, function(pt) {     pred_pos <- probs >= pt     tp <- sum(y == 1 & pred_pos)     fp <- sum(y == 0 & pred_pos)     (tp / n) - (fp / n) * (pt / (1 - pt))   }, numeric(1)) }  nb_all <- prev - (1 - prev) * (thresholds / (1 - thresholds))  dca_df <- bind_rows(   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$adj_nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Adjusted NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$bw_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Backward elimination"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$lasso_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "LASSO"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = nb_all,     label = "Treat all"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = 0,     label = "Treat none"   ) )  # Compute costs per method cost_nb <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_nb, grouped_costs) cost_adj <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_adj, grouped_costs) cost_bw <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(bw$names.kept, grouped_costs) cost_lasso <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(lasso_kept, grouped_costs)  dca_df <- dca_df |>   mutate(     cost = case_when(       label == "NB Model" ~ cost_nb,       label == "Adjusted NB Model" ~ cost_adj,       label == "Backward elimination" ~ cost_bw,       label == "LASSO" ~ cost_lasso,       TRUE ~ 0     ),     adj_net_benefit = net_benefit - cost   )  model_labels <- c(   "NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination",   "LASSO", "Treat all", "Treat none" ) dca_df$label <- factor(dca_df$label, levels = model_labels)  # Raw NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Decision Threshold", y = "Net Benefit", color = "Strategy",        linetype = "Strategy") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))  # Adjusted NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = adj_net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(     x = "Decision Threshold",     y = "Harm-adjusted Net Benefit",     color = "Strategy",     linetype = "Strategy"   ) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$adj_net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
code: # Clean up simple illustration objects to avoid namespace conflicts rm(list = setdiff(ls(), lsf.str()))  set.seed(8156) n_train <- 3000 n_test <- 1500 n_total <- n_train + n_test  # Clinical history (free) patient_age <- round(rnorm(n_total, mean = 68, sd = 12)) prior_admission <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.30) specialist_centre <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.45) symptom_severity <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.40)  # Cardiac imaging (moderate cost) ejection_fraction <- pmin(75, pmax(10, rnorm(n_total, 42, 14))) wall_thickness <- pmin(1, pmax(0.05, rbeta(n_total, 2, 4))) valve_abnormalities <- rpois(n_total, lambda = 0.8) pericardial_effusion <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.12) chamber_dilation <- rbinom(n_total, 1, 0.22)  # Blood biomarker (higher cost) nt_probnp <- round(pmax(20, rlnorm(n_total, meanlog = 6.5, sdlog = 1.4)))  # True model — strong signal for key predictors lp <- -16.0 +   0.07 * patient_age +   3.0 * prior_admission +   -0.12 * ejection_fraction +   10.0 * wall_thickness +   1.5 * valve_abnormalities +   2.5 * pericardial_effusion +   1.8 * chamber_dilation +   0.9 * log2(nt_probnp) +   # Weak / null predictors:   0.03 * specialist_centre +   0.02 * symptom_severity  readmitted <- rbinom(n_total, 1, plogis(lp))  clinical_df <- data.frame(   readmitted, patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission,   specialist_centre, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities,   pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, symptom_severity,   wall_thickness )  training_data <- clinical_df[1:n_train, ] test_data <- clinical_df[(n_train + 1):n_total, ]  sprintf("Training prevalence: %.1f%%", 100 * mean(training_data$readmitted)) [1] "Training prevalence: 34.5%" sprintf("Test prevalence: %.1f%%", 100 * mean(test_data$readmitted)) [1] "Test prevalence: 35.8%"

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Costs defined grouped costs. group cost added predictor group included model.
code: prevalence <- mean(test_data$readmitted)  grouped_costs <- list(   history = list(     cost = 0,     vars = c("patient_age", "prior_admission", "specialist_centre",              "symptom_severity")   ),   imaging = list(     cost = prevalence * 0.03,     vars = c(       "ejection_fraction", "wall_thickness",       "valve_abnormalities", "pericardial_effusion", "chamber_dilation"     )   ),   biomarker = list(     cost = prevalence * 0.08,     vars = c("nt_probnp")   ) )

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: Table 2: Best model exhaustive search Table 3: models exhaustive search Figure 3: Variable importance plot (exhaustive search) Figure 3 shows variable importance based average Net Benefit contribution. Predictors ranked least important. Figure 4: subset plot (Net Benefit) Figure 5: subset plot (Adjusted Net Benefit) Figure 4 Figure 5 show -subset plots. point represents model. heatmap indicates predictors included. Note difference ranking costs accounted .
code: vars <- names(training_data)  start_time <- Sys.time() exhaustive_results <- nb_varsel(   data = training_data,   outcome_var = "readmitted",   costs = grouped_costs,   thresholds = seq(0.01, 0.2, by = 0.01),   include_interactions = FALSE,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "exhaustive",   allow_parallel = TRUE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE ) end_time <- Sys.time()  sprintf("Exhaustive search took: %s", format(end_time - start_time)) [1] "Exhaustive search took: 4.199386 mins" exhaustive_results$best_model_stats |>   select(Model, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit) |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Net Benefit",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. Net Benefit"   ) all_models <- exhaustive_results$all_models all_models$Rank_NB <- rank(-all_models$Avg_Net_Benefit, ties.method = "min") all_models$Rank_adj_NB <- rank(-all_models$Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, ties.method = "min")  tbl_data <- all_models |>   select(     Rank_NB, Rank_adj_NB, Model, AUC, Brier,     Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit   )  tbl <- tbl_data |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Rank_NB = "Rank (NB)",     Rank_adj_NB = "Rank (Adj. NB)",     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. NB",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. NB"   )  if (knitr::is_html_output()) {   tbl <- tbl |>     opt_interactive(       use_pagination = TRUE,       page_size_default = 10,       use_filters = TRUE,       use_compact_mode = TRUE     ) } tbl VIF_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = round(nrow(exhaustive_results$all_models) * 0.1) ) all_subset_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = 7,   size_dot = 1 )  all_subset_plot(   exhaustive_results$all_models,   filter = 7,   size_dot = 1,   metric = "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit",   y_axis = "Adjusted Net Benefit" )

text: Table 4: Groupwise selection: best model Figure 6: Variable importance (groupwise selection)
code: start_time <- Sys.time() groupwise <- nb_varsel(   data = training_data,   outcome_var = "readmitted",   costs = grouped_costs,   thresholds = seq(0.01, 0.2, by = 0.01),   include_interactions = FALSE,   cv_folds = 5,   mode = "groupwise",   allow_parallel = TRUE,   permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE,   group_size = 1,   verbose = TRUE ) end_time <- Sys.time() sprintf("Groupwise search took: %s", format(end_time - start_time)) [1] "Groupwise search took: 17.77182 secs" groupwise$best_model_stats |>   select(Model, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit) |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(     columns = c("AUC", "Brier", "Total_Cost", "Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit", "Avg_Net_Benefit"),     decimals = 3   ) |>   cols_label(     Model = "Included predictors",     AUC = "AUC",     Brier = "Brier Score",     Avg_Net_Benefit = "Avg. NB",     Total_Cost = "Total Cost",     Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit = "Avg. Adj. NB"   ) VIF_plot(groupwise$all_models)

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: compare NB-based variable selection backward elimination (Wald statistic) LASSO (L1-penalised logistic regression). Table 5: AUC test data variable selection method Figure 7: Decision curve analysis comparing net benefit across methods. Figure 8: Decision curve analysis comparing harm-adjusted net benefit. Figure 7 shows standard decision curve analysis. Figure 8 shows curves subtracting method’s test costs, highlighting advantage cost-aware variable selection.
code: preds <- setdiff(vars, "readmitted")  # --- Backward elimination --- fmla_full <- reformulate(termlabels = preds, response = "readmitted") model_full <- lrm(fmla_full, data = training_data) bw <- fastbw(model_full) model_bw <- lrm(   reformulate(termlabels = bw$names.kept, response = "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) test_data$bw_pred <- plogis(predict(model_bw, newdata = test_data))  # --- LASSO --- x_train <- as.matrix(training_data[, preds]) y_train <- training_data$readmitted x_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, preds])  set.seed(4738) cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1, family = "binomial") lasso_coefs <- coef(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.1se") lasso_kept <- rownames(lasso_coefs)[as.vector(lasso_coefs != 0)] lasso_kept <- setdiff(lasso_kept, "(Intercept)") test_data$lasso_pred <- as.vector(   plogis(predict(cv_lasso, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.1se")) )  # --- NB models --- preds_best_nb <- str_trim(   str_split(     all_models |> arrange(-Avg_Net_Benefit) |> slice(1) |> pull(Model),     ","   )[[1]] ) preds_best_adj <- str_trim(   str_split(exhaustive_results$best_model_stats$Model, ",")[[1]] )  model_nb <- lrm(   reformulate(preds_best_nb, "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) model_adj_nb <- lrm(   reformulate(preds_best_adj, "readmitted"),   data = training_data ) test_data$nb_pred <- plogis(predict(model_nb, newdata = test_data)) test_data$adj_nb_pred <- plogis(predict(model_adj_nb, newdata = test_data)) sprintf("NB model retains: %s", paste(preds_best_nb, collapse = ", ")) [1] "NB model retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, specialist_centre, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("Adjusted NB model retains: %s", paste(preds_best_adj, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Adjusted NB model retains: patient_age, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("Backward elimination retains: %s", paste(bw$names.kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "Backward elimination retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, wall_thickness" sprintf("LASSO retains: %s", paste(lasso_kept, collapse = ", ")) [1] "LASSO retains: patient_age, nt_probnp, prior_admission, ejection_fraction, valve_abnormalities, pericardial_effusion, chamber_dilation, symptom_severity, wall_thickness" auc_results <- data.frame(   Method = c("NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination", "LASSO"),   AUC = c(     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$adj_nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$bw_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$lasso_pred))   ),   n_predictors = c(     length(preds_best_nb),     length(preds_best_adj),     length(bw$names.kept),     length(lasso_kept)   ) )  auc_results |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(columns = "AUC", decimals = 3) |>   cols_label(n_predictors = "N predictors") thresholds <- seq(0.01, 0.30, by = 0.005) n_test_obs <- nrow(test_data) y_test <- test_data$readmitted prev <- mean(y_test)  calc_nb <- function(probs, y, thresholds) {   n <- length(y)   vapply(thresholds, function(pt) {     pred_pos <- probs >= pt     tp <- sum(y == 1 & pred_pos)     fp <- sum(y == 0 & pred_pos)     (tp / n) - (fp / n) * (pt / (1 - pt))   }, numeric(1)) }  nb_all <- prev - (1 - prev) * (thresholds / (1 - thresholds))  dca_df <- bind_rows(   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$adj_nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Adjusted NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$bw_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Backward elimination"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$lasso_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "LASSO"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = nb_all,     label = "Treat all"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = 0,     label = "Treat none"   ) )  # Compute costs per method cost_nb <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_nb, grouped_costs) cost_adj <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_adj, grouped_costs) cost_bw <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(bw$names.kept, grouped_costs) cost_lasso <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(lasso_kept, grouped_costs)  dca_df <- dca_df |>   mutate(     cost = case_when(       label == "NB Model" ~ cost_nb,       label == "Adjusted NB Model" ~ cost_adj,       label == "Backward elimination" ~ cost_bw,       label == "LASSO" ~ cost_lasso,       TRUE ~ 0     ),     adj_net_benefit = net_benefit - cost   )  model_labels <- c(   "NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination",   "LASSO", "Treat all", "Treat none" ) dca_df$label <- factor(dca_df$label, levels = model_labels)  # Raw NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Decision Threshold", y = "Net Benefit", color = "Strategy",        linetype = "Strategy") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))  # Adjusted NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = adj_net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(     x = "Decision Threshold",     y = "Harm-adjusted Net Benefit",     color = "Strategy",     linetype = "Strategy"   ) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$adj_net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel

code: auc_results <- data.frame(   Method = c("NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination", "LASSO"),   AUC = c(     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$adj_nb_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$bw_pred)),     as.numeric(pROC::auc(test_data$readmitted, test_data$lasso_pred))   ),   n_predictors = c(     length(preds_best_nb),     length(preds_best_adj),     length(bw$names.kept),     length(lasso_kept)   ) )  auc_results |>   gt() |>   fmt_number(columns = "AUC", decimals = 3) |>   cols_label(n_predictors = "N predictors")

Figure 8 shows curves subtracting method’s test costs, highlighting advantage cost-aware variable selection.
code: thresholds <- seq(0.01, 0.30, by = 0.005) n_test_obs <- nrow(test_data) y_test <- test_data$readmitted prev <- mean(y_test)  calc_nb <- function(probs, y, thresholds) {   n <- length(y)   vapply(thresholds, function(pt) {     pred_pos <- probs >= pt     tp <- sum(y == 1 & pred_pos)     fp <- sum(y == 0 & pred_pos)     (tp / n) - (fp / n) * (pt / (1 - pt))   }, numeric(1)) }  nb_all <- prev - (1 - prev) * (thresholds / (1 - thresholds))  dca_df <- bind_rows(   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$adj_nb_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Adjusted NB Model"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$bw_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "Backward elimination"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = calc_nb(test_data$lasso_pred, y_test, thresholds),     label = "LASSO"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = nb_all,     label = "Treat all"   ),   data.frame(     threshold = thresholds,     net_benefit = 0,     label = "Treat none"   ) )  # Compute costs per method cost_nb <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_nb, grouped_costs) cost_adj <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(preds_best_adj, grouped_costs) cost_bw <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(bw$names.kept, grouped_costs) cost_lasso <- NBvarsel:::calculate_model_cost(lasso_kept, grouped_costs)  dca_df <- dca_df |>   mutate(     cost = case_when(       label == "NB Model" ~ cost_nb,       label == "Adjusted NB Model" ~ cost_adj,       label == "Backward elimination" ~ cost_bw,       label == "LASSO" ~ cost_lasso,       TRUE ~ 0     ),     adj_net_benefit = net_benefit - cost   )  model_labels <- c(   "NB Model", "Adjusted NB Model", "Backward elimination",   "LASSO", "Treat all", "Treat none" ) dca_df$label <- factor(dca_df$label, levels = model_labels)  # Raw NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Decision Threshold", y = "Net Benefit", color = "Strategy",        linetype = "Strategy") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))  # Adjusted NB ggplot(dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = adj_net_benefit, color = label, linetype = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +   theme_classic(base_size = 12) +   labs(     x = "Decision Threshold",     y = "Harm-adjusted Net Benefit",     color = "Strategy",     linetype = "Strategy"   ) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.30), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$adj_net_benefit) * 1.05)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "#0072B5", "Adjusted NB Model" = "#BC3C29",     "Backward elimination" = "#E18727", "LASSO" = "#20854E",     "Treat all" = "black", "Treat none" = "black"   )) +   scale_linetype_manual(values = c(     "NB Model" = "solid", "Adjusted NB Model" = "solid",     "Backward elimination" = "solid", "LASSO" = "solid",     "Treat all" = "dashed", "Treat none" = "dotted"   ))

title: Variable Selection via Net Benefit with NBvarsel
text: vignette demonstrated NBvarsel workflow: nb_varsel() identifies optimal predictor subsets maximising cross-validated Net Benefit, optionally adjusted test costs. VIF_plot() visualises permutation-based variable importance. all_subset_plot() provides comprehensive view model performance predictor inclusion across evaluated combinations. External validation selected models can performed using standard discrimination metrics AUC. key insight statistically best-performing model may clinically useful predictor costs taken account. Cost-aware variable selection via Net Benefit can lead simpler, cheaper models maintain improve clinical utility.

what: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
text: Variable selection binary outcome prediction models using cross-validated Net Benefit optimization criterion.

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
text: NBvarsel evaluates predictor subsets contribution clinical decision-making (Net Benefit), rather purely statistical performance metrics like AUC. supports: Exhaustive groupwise (backward elimination) search strategies Predictor costs (per-variable grouped) Restricted cubic splines continuous predictors Interaction terms Permutation importance scores Parallel computation

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
text: can install development version NBvarsel GitHub: Alternatively, install source tarball (.tar.gz):
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("LasaiBarrenada/NB_varsel") install.packages("NBvarsel_0.1.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
code: library(NBvarsel)  # Simulate data set.seed(42) n <- 500 df <- data.frame(   X1 = rnorm(n), X2 = rbinom(n, 1, 0.7),   X3 = rnorm(n), X4 = rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) ) df$Y <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(2 * df$X1 + 1.5 * df$X2 + 0.2 * df$X3))  # Define predictor costs harms <- c(X1 = 0.1, X2 = 0.05, X3 = 0.1, X4 = 0.0001)  # Run exhaustive variable selection result <- nb_varsel(   data = df,   outcome_var = "Y",   costs = harms,   mode = "exhaustive",   splines = FALSE,   permutation = TRUE,   allow_parallel = FALSE )  # Best model (highest cost-adjusted Net Benefit) result$best_model_stats  # All evaluated models, sorted by Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit result$all_models

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit
code: # Two-panel plot: metric overview + predictor inclusion heatmap all_subset_plot(result$all_models)  # Customise colors all_subset_plot(   result$all_models,   highlight_color = "steelblue",   tile_color = "#E64B35" )  # Permutation importance bar chart VIF_plot(result$all_models, color = "darkgreen")

what: NBvarsel: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — NBvarsel-package
title: NBvarsel: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — NBvarsel-package
text: Performs exhaustive groupwise (backward elimination) variable selection binary outcome prediction models using cross-validated Net Benefit optimization criterion. Supports predictor costs, restricted cubic splines, interaction terms, permutation importance, parallel computation. Includes visualizations model comparison variable importance.

title: NBvarsel: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — NBvarsel-package
text: Maintainer: Lasai Barreñada lasai.barrenadataleb@kuleuven.(ORCID)

title: Permutation Importance Bar Plot — VIF_plot
text: Creates horizontal bar chart showing average permutation importance (delta Net Benefit) predictor across evaluated models.

title: Permutation Importance Bar Plot — VIF_plot
code: VIF_plot(all_models, filter = NULL, color = "#2A6EBB")

title: Permutation Importance Bar Plot — VIF_plot
text: all_models Data frame returned all_models element nb_varsel(). Must include VIF_* columns (generated permutation = TRUE). filter Integer NULL. specified, top filter models (Avg_Net_Benefit) used compute average importance. Defaults NULL (use models). color Character string. Fill color bars. Defaults "#2A6EBB".

title: Permutation Importance Bar Plot — VIF_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Permutation Importance Bar Plot — VIF_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- nb_varsel(   data = df, outcome_var = "Y", costs = harms,   mode = "exhaustive", permutation = TRUE,   splines = FALSE, allow_parallel = FALSE ) VIF_plot(result$all_models) } # }

title: All-Subset Model Comparison Plot — all_subset_plot
text: Creates two-panel figure: top panel shows performance metric across evaluated models (ordered number predictors metric value), bottom panel shows heatmap predictors included model.

title: All-Subset Model Comparison Plot — all_subset_plot
code: all_subset_plot(   all_models,   metric = "Avg_Net_Benefit",   y_axis = "Average Net Benefit",   filter = 5,   size_dot = 3,   highlight_color = "red",   tile_color = "#2A6EBB",   p1_theme = NULL,   p2_theme = NULL )

title: All-Subset Model Comparison Plot — all_subset_plot
text: all_models Data frame returned all_models element nb_varsel(). metric Character string. Column name all_models plot y-axis. Defaults "Avg_Net_Benefit". y_axis Character string. Label y-axis top panel. Defaults "Average Net Benefit". filter Integer. 100 models, keep top filter models per number predictors. Defaults 5. size_dot Numeric. Point size metric plot. Defaults 3. highlight_color Character string. Color dashed lines marking best model. Defaults "red". tile_color Character string. Fill color "present" tiles heatmap panel. Defaults "#2A6EBB". p1_theme ggplot2::theme() object add top panel, NULL. p2_theme ggplot2::theme() object add bottom panel, NULL.

title: All-Subset Model Comparison Plot — all_subset_plot
text: patchwork::patchwork object (two stacked ggplot panels).

title: All-Subset Model Comparison Plot — all_subset_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- nb_varsel(   data = df, outcome_var = "Y", costs = harms,   mode = "exhaustive", splines = FALSE, allow_parallel = FALSE ) all_subset_plot(result$all_models) } # }

what: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
text: Performs exhaustive groupwise (backward elimination) variable selection binary outcome prediction models. Models evaluated using cross-validated Net Benefit, optionally adjusted predictor costs.

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
code: nb_varsel(   data,   outcome_var,   costs = NULL,   thresholds = seq(0.05, 0.3, by = 0.01),   include_interactions = FALSE,   mode = c("exhaustive", "groupwise"),   group_size = 2,   cv_folds = 5,   seed = 123,   verbose = TRUE,   allow_parallel = TRUE,   permutation = FALSE,   splines = TRUE,   n_knots = 3 )

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
text: data data frame containing predictors outcome variable. outcome_var Character string naming binary outcome column (coded 0/1 two-level factor). costs Predictor costs. Can : NULL (default): costs. named numeric vector: per-predictor costs (e.g., c(X1 = 0.1, X2 = 0.05)). unnamed scalar: cost applied every predictor. list groups, elements vars (character vector) cost (numeric). group cost added variables included. thresholds Numeric vector decision thresholds compute Net Benefit. Defaults seq(0.05, 0.3, = 0.01). include_interactions Logical. TRUE, models interaction terms ((X1 + X2 + ...)^2) also evaluated (exhaustive mode ). Defaults FALSE. mode Either "exhaustive" (evaluate predictor combinations) "groupwise" (backward elimination removing group_size variables time). Defaults "exhaustive". group_size Integer. Number variables consider removing step groupwise mode. Defaults 2. cv_folds Integer. Number cross-validation folds. Defaults 5. seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. Defaults 123. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Defaults TRUE. allow_parallel Logical. Use parallel computing via foreach::foreach() doParallel::registerDoParallel(). Defaults TRUE. permutation Logical. Compute permutation importance scores predictor. Defaults FALSE. splines Logical. Apply restricted cubic splines (via rms::rcs()) continuous predictors. Defaults TRUE. n_knots Integer. Number knots restricted cubic splines. Defaults 3.

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
text: list two elements: best_model_stats one-row data frame describing top-performing model. all_models data frame one row per evaluated model, containing columns Model, n_Preds, AUC, Brier, Total_Cost, Avg_Adj_Net_Benefit, Avg_Net_Benefit, (permutation = TRUE) VIF_* columns predictor.

title: Variable Selection via Cross-Validated Net Benefit — nb_varsel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) n <- 500 df <- data.frame(   X1 = rnorm(n), X2 = rbinom(n, 1, 0.7),   X3 = rnorm(n), X4 = rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) ) df$Y <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(2 * df$X1 + 1.5 * df$X2))  harms <- c(X1 = 0.1, X2 = 0.05, X3 = 0.1, X4 = 0.0001)  result <- nb_varsel(   data = df, outcome_var = "Y", costs = harms,   mode = "exhaustive", splines = FALSE,   allow_parallel = FALSE ) result$best_model_stats } # }

title: NBvarsel 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Added tutorial vignette (nb-varsel-tutorial) demonstrating full workflow fabricated clinical data, including comparison backward elimination LASSO. nb_varsel() performs exhaustive groupwise variable selection binary outcome models using cross-validated Net Benefit, support predictor costs (per-variable grouped), restricted cubic splines, interaction terms, permutation importance, parallel computation. all_subset_plot() creates two-panel figure showing model performance predictor inclusion across evaluated models. Supports customisable colors via highlight_color tile_color arguments. VIF_plot() creates horizontal bar chart average permutation importance (delta Net Benefit) per predictor. Supports customisable color argument.
